pi_items.source: Downloaded from https://wiki.wireshark.org/uploads/__moin_import__/attachments/SampleCaptures/200722_tcp_anon.pcapng; verified as valid pcapng file (12,800 bytes)
Task specification matches expected output path per task.json description field
Verified via tshark analysis; stream 0 exists in PCAP file with complete TCP handshake
tshark -r pcapng -Y 'tcp.stream==0 && tcp.flags.syn==1 && tcp.flags.ack==0' -T fields -e tcp.options.mss_val
tshark -r pcapng -Y 'tcp.stream==0 && tcp.flags.syn==1 && tcp.flags.ack==1' -T fields -e tcp.options.mss_val
tshark -r pcapng -Y 'tcp.stream==0 && tcp.flags.syn==1 && tcp.flags.ack==0' -T fields -e tcp.options.wscale.shift
tshark -r pcapng -Y 'tcp.stream==0 && tcp.flags.syn==1 && tcp.flags.ack==1' -T fields -e tcp.options.wscale.shift
tshark -r pcapng -Y 'tcp.stream==0' -T fields -e tcp.len | sort -rn | head -1; max tcp.len in stream 0 is 6 bytes (packet 4: PSH+ACK with 6-byte payload)
TSO/Jumbo Frames detection: max_payload (6) is NOT greater than negotiated_mss (min(1460,1460)=1460), therefore TSO=NO

privileged_info_summary: The task analyzes TCP Stream 0 in the file 200722_tcp_anon.pcapng, which is a real packet capture from Wireshark's official sample captures repository. Stream 0 contains a complete TCP handshake with Client MSS=1460, Server MSS=1460, Client Window Scale=8 (window size 256), Server Window Scale=7 (window size 128). The maximum TCP payload size in Stream 0 is 6 bytes. TSO/Jumbo Frames are not present, as the maximum payload (6 bytes) does not exceed the negotiated MSS (1460 bytes).
